FAMILY FOUNDATION, BRUCE BUCKLEY'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL

Appearances: Nelson Goodell of The Goodell Law Firm (415.495.3950) for plaintiff and responding party; Jonathan Fink of Wright Finlay & Zak (949.477.5050) appearing via Zoom for defendants and moving parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. This motion has been previously continued from December 11, 2024, calendar for supplemental briefing to address compliance with CCP 405.22 as the record did not indicate any filing of the Notice of Lis Pendens, let alone a filing "immediately following recordation." In the supplemental declaration Plaintiff admitted that no such filing had been made and argued that failure to comply with CCP 405.22 does not render the Lis Pendens defective. Plaintiff has not filed a notice of lis pendens with the Court. The lis pendens is now expunged as Plaintiff failed to show probable validity of a real property claim. Defendants' requests for fees to be paid by the Plaintiff within 30 days of notice of entry of order is granted in the amount of $7,046.50. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order.
